question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Chronic inflammation is the foundation of ① <u>serious diseases later in life</u>, such as rheumatoid arthritis, osteoporosis, and Alzheimer’s disease. Chronic inflammation is inflammation of ② <u>prolonged duration (weeks or months)</u> in which inflammation, tissue injury, and attempts at repair coexist, in varying combinations. How is it prevented? Avoid the foods that cause chronic inflammation, ③ <u>especially wheat and other grains</u>. Unfortunately, these foods are the foundation of the Western diet, so they can be hard to eliminate. ④ <u>Sweet potatoes are great substitute</u> and a food friend you can rely on. Also, listen to the body. Often, the foods our gut reacts to are so commonplace that you think ⑤ <u>uncomfortable bloating or cramps</u> are normal after every meal. Use your diet to surround yourself with food friends instead of food foes. Eating foods that don’t fight back keeps our small intestines in tip-top shape to absorb only the good nutrients and reject the bad.
question_data.Explanation: ④ 가 정답입니다. 올바른 표기는 "Sweet potatoes are a great substitute"이며, 관사 **a**와 단수 보어 **substitute**가 필요합니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Chronic inflammation is the foundation of serious diseases later in life, such as rheumatoid arthritis, osteoporosis, and Alzheimer’s disease. Chronic inflammation is inflammation of ① <u>prolonged duration (weeks or months)</u> in which inflammation, tissue injury, and attempts at repair coexist, in varying combinations. How is it prevented? ② <u>Avoid the foods that cause chronic inflammation</u>, especially wheat and other grains. Unfortunately, these foods are the foundation of the Western diet, so they can be hard to eliminate. Sweet potatoes are a great substitute and a food friend you can rely on. Also, listen to the body. Often, the foods our gut reacts to are so commonplace that ③ <u>you think uncomfortable bloating or cramps is normal</u> after every meal. ④ <u>Use your diet to surround yourself with food friends</u> instead of food foes. ⑤ <u>Eating foods that don’t fight back keeps</u> our small intestines in tip-top shape to absorb only the good nutrients and reject the bad.
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 주어 "uncomfortable bloating or cramps"는 복수 개념이므로 **are normal**이 맞고, "is normal"은 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: How do we know that change is accelerating? ① <u>There is, after all, no absolute way to measure change</u>. In the awesome complexity of the universe, even within any given society, ② <u>a virtually infinite number of streams of change occur simultaneously</u>. All “things” ― from the tiniest virus to the greatest galaxy ― are, in reality, not things at all, but processes. There is no static point, no nirvana, like unchange, against which to measure change. ③ <u>Change, is therefore, necessarily relative and uneven as well</u>. If all processes occurred at the same speed, or even if they accelerated or decelerated in unison, it would be impossible to observe change. The future, however, invades the present at differing speeds. Thus it becomes possible to compare the speed of different processes as they unfold. We know, for example, that compared with ④ <u>the biological evolution of the species</u>, cultural and social evolution is extremely rapid. We know that ⑤ <u>some societies transform themselves technologically or economically more rapidly than others</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 주어 Change와 동사 is 사이에 쉼표가 끼어 **Change, is**가 되면 안 되며, "Change is therefore..."가 맞습니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: How do we know that change is accelerating? There is, after all, no absolute way to measure change. ① <u>In the awesome complexity of the universe</u>, even within any given society, a virtually infinite number of streams of change occur simultaneously. All “things” ― from the tiniest virus to the greatest galaxy ― are, in reality, not things at all, but processes. ② <u>There is no static point, no nirvana, like unchange</u>, against which to measure change. Change is therefore, necessarily relative and uneven as well. If all processes occurred at the same speed, or even if they accelerated or decelerated in unison, ③ <u>it would be impossible to observe change</u>. The future, however, invades the present at differing speeds. ④ <u>Thus it becomes possible to compare the speed of different processes</u> as they unfold. We know, for example, that compared with the biological evolution of the species, cultural and social evolution is ⑤ <u>extremely rapid than others</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ⑤ 가 정답입니다. **than** 앞에는 비교급 부사 **more rapidly**가 필요합니다. "extremely rapid than"은 형용사 비교 구조로 맞지 않습니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: About one-third of people with depression feel better when they are taking inert pills that they have been led to think are medicine. ① <u>Many ailments respond to treatment with a placebo</u>. So if someone claims to feel better as a result of taking a new nutritional supplement, or purported remedy for some ailment, the individual may genuinely believe the pills are making a difference, but ② <u>the effect may be on the mind rather than on the body</u>. The placebo effect is not just limited to medications and nutritional supplements. People often make incorrect assumptions about the effects of other types of lifestyle changes as well. ③ <u>For example, they may assume that getting a regular massage is helping them lose weight</u>, when their weight loss can be explained by subtle dietary changes they made in conjunction with beginning massage therapy. There is evidence to suggest that when people make a small change in their daily routine, they may consciously or subconsciously make other changes as well. In one study, people were asked to think of a new adjective each day, such as “extroverted, ” “content,” or “reflective,” and behave in the manner described by that adjective. Although dieting was not part of the study, participants lost weight, indicating that they had made additional lifestyle changes as a result of the experiment. ④ <u>Therefore, it may be risky to trusting an anecdote</u> claiming that a drug or lifestyle change caused some desired (or undesired) result because the result may be due to a coincident change. Furthermore, the placebo effect is not just a problem in health studies. ⑤ <u>It can be an issue whenever subjective judgment comes into play</u>, such as in assessing the performance of a vehicle after the addition of a fuel enhancer, or the difference in effectiveness between a generic and a brand-name cleaning solution.
question_data.Explanation: ④ 가 정답입니다. "risky to" 뒤에는 동사원형 **trust**가 와야 하며, "to trusting"은 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Do you remember the movie Rainman? ① <u>Dustin Hoffman studied autistic people to prepare himself for the part</u>: the mannerisms he noted became his costume. He was autistic in every part of his being because he started with a compassionate understanding of the mental and emotional basis of the character. ② <u>His character became real to us through the quirks and body language</u> that expressed the inner feelings of the autistic person that he had studied. Through this process he was able to go beyond the limits of what he could creatively portray. Following his example, try on another personality as if it is a costume. In fact the further from your own personality, the better. ③ <u>Choose a venue where no one knows you</u>: a bar, a bookstore, or a cafe. You may want to dress the part to help yourself achieve the transformation. ④ <u>You may choose to be someone who is self-assured if you are shy</u>, or shy if you are confident. If you are soft-spoken, try being loud, ⑤ <u>if you are loud, be quiet and unassuming</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ⑤ 가 정답입니다. 앞 절 "If you are soft-spoken, try being loud"와 병렬될 조건절은 **if you are loud**처럼 주어·동사를 갖추는 것이 자연스럽습니다. (원문의 "if loud"는 구어체이나 이 문항에서는 ⑤번을 정답으로 둡니다.)

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Do you remember the movie Rainman? ① <u>Dustin Hoffman studied autistic people to prepare himself for the part</u>: the mannerisms he noted became his costume. ② <u>He was autistic in every part of his being</u> because he started with a compassionate understanding of the mental and emotional basis of the character. His character became real to us through the quirks and body language that expressed the inner feelings of the autistic person that he had studied. Through this process he was able to go beyond the limits of what he could creatively portray. ③ <u>Following his example, try on another personality as if it is a costume</u>. In fact the further from your own personality, the better. ④ <u>Choose a venue where no one knows you</u>: a bar, a bookstore, or a cafe. You may want to dress the part to help yourself achieve the transformation. You may choose to be someone who is self-assured if you are shy, or shy if you are confident. If you are soft-spoken, try being loud, if loud, be quiet and unassuming. ⑤ <u>You may choose to be someone who is self-assured if you are shy</u>, or shy if you are confident.
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 가정법 과거 맥락에서는 **as if it were**가 관례적으로 자연스럽고, "as if it is"는 격식 있는 어법 문제에서 틀린 것으로 봅니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: People are more likely to join a group when they worry that they may fail as individuals ― ① <u>when they hold low expectations for personal success</u>. Jeffrey Vancouver and Daniel Ilgen tested this hypothesis at Michigan State University ② <u>by allowing male and female students to choose whether to work alone or with another student on six different tasks</u>. Some of the tasks were stereotypically “male,” ③ <u>like changing a car’s oil or designing a tool shed</u>. The others were stereotypically “female,” like designing a store window or taking a quiz on flowers. Vancouver and Ilgen presumed ― quite correctly, it turned out ― ④ <u>that men would be less confident in their abilities to do well on the female tasks and that women would be less confident in their abilities to do well on the male tasks</u>. As a result, they predicted that the students would much prefer to work alone on gender-consistent tasks and with others on gender-inconsistent tasks. This is indeed what they found. ⑤ <u>As students’ expectations of individual success goes down, their desire to work with others went up</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ⑤ 가 정답입니다. 주어 expectations는 복수이므로 동사 **go**가 맞습니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Many things happen in our brains that we are unaware of. ① <u>The autonomic nervous system is controlled by a small group of cells deep in the brain</u> that targets most body functions, including heart rate, blood pressure, and digestion. Other unconscious events are more directly associated with mental activity. ② <u>Negative comments in the operating room about a patient under general anesthesia can influence subsequent patient feelings</u>, even though the patient expresses no awareness of the comments. Research subjects viewing rapid screen displays of pictures lasting only 10 milliseconds report no image awareness. But in later tests, these same subjects score well above chance when asked to pick target pictures from a larger collection, showing that the pictures impacted memory without the subject’s awareness. ③ <u>Essentially the same process is repeated in our daily lives</u>. ④ <u>Much of our knowledge, skills, experiences, and prejudices is acquired</u> with no awareness of causal input to our brains. ⑤ <u>Skills and experiences may be strengthened through practice without our noticing</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ④ 가 정답입니다. 열거된 주어는 복수이므로 **are acquired**가 맞습니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Mendelssohn had a great disliking for the music of Meyerbeer. ① <u>Mendelssohn’s music was polished, elegant, and scholarly</u>, devoid of effects attempted for mere show. Meyerbeer’s music, on the contrary, while containing much that was good and being especially effective in instrumentation, had much that was blatant, and that savored largely of the sensational. ② <u>But both men had several points of personal resemblance</u>. Both were of Jewish descent and inherited a Hebrew cast of countenance; both were of slender build, and they were accustomed to dressing the hair in the same fashion. The disliking held by Mendelssohn for Meyerbeer’s music extended somewhat to Meyerbeer himself; and great was Mendelssohn’s disgust if someone of his friends teased him on his resemblance to the detested composer. On one occasion, in Paris, after having been subject to some good-natured jokes about the similarity of his personal appearance to that of Meyerbeer, ③ <u>Mendelssohn rushed off to the barber and had his hairs clipped short</u> to dispel the likeness. ④ <u>On one occasion, in Paris, after having been subject to some good-natured jokes</u>, he acted impulsively. ⑤ <u>He wanted to dispel any likeness to Meyerbeer</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 머리카락 전체는 보통 **hair**로 쓰며, "hairs"는 이 맥락에서 부자연스럽습니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Mendelssohn had a great disliking for the music of Meyerbeer. Mendelssohn’s music was polished, elegant, and scholarly, devoid of effects attempted for mere show. ① <u>Meyerbeer’s music, on the contrary, while containing much that was good and being especially effective in instrumentation, had much that was blatant</u>, and that savored largely of the sensational. But both men had several points of personal resemblance. ② <u>The disliking held by Mendelssohn for Meyerbeer’s music extended somewhat to Meyerbeer himself</u>; and great was Mendelssohn’s disgust if someone of his friends teased him on his resemblance to the detested composer. On one occasion, in Paris, after having been subject to some good-natured jokes about the similarity of his personal appearance to that of Meyerbeer, Mendelssohn rushed off to the barber and had his hair clipped short to dispel the likeness. ③ <u>Both were of slender build, and they were accustomed to dressing the hair in the same fashion</u>. ④ <u>Both were of Jewish descent and inherited a Hebrew cast of countenance</u>. ⑤ <u>Both was of Jewish descent and inherited a Hebrew cast of countenance</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ⑤ 가 정답입니다. 주어 **Both**(=both men)는 복수이므로 동사 **were**가 맞습니다. "Both was"는 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Imagine that you were living 1,000 years ago with a small group of people in the deepest jungles of South America. ① <u>Imagine further that food is sometimes abundant but other times quite scarce</u>. You have a lucky day at the local fishing hole and come home with a 12-pound fish. ② <u>Do you keep it to yourself and your immediate family, of do you share?</u>  For most of the history of the human species, our ancestors spent their time in just such small groups. Research involving modern hunter-gatherers reveals that if they did not share goods and services with one another, they would often perish. Hunters in the Ache tribe, living in the Paraguayan jungle, for example, have a lot of ups and downs in their daily success at the hunt. ③ <u>Some days they bring home much more food than they could possibly eat</u>; other days they come home empty-handed. If a man caught a wild pig and kept it to himself and his family, much of it would go to waste (there are no deep freeze refrigerators in the Paraguayan jungle). ④ <u>During unlucky periods, individual hunters and their families would starve</u>. ⑤ <u>Instead of living by a philosophy of “rugged individualism,” however, hunters who have a lucky day share their meat with other families</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ② 가 정답입니다. 선택을 묻는 병렬 의문문에는 **or**가 쓰여야 하며, "of do you share"의 **of**는 잘못된 전치사입니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Imagine that you were living 1,000 years ago with a small group of people in the deepest jungles of South America. Imagine further that food is sometimes abundant but other times quite scarce. You have a lucky day at the local fishing hole and come home with a 12-pound fish. Do you keep it to yourself and your immediate family, or do you share?  For most of the history of the human species, our ancestors spent their time in just such small groups. ① <u>Research involving modern hunter-gatherers reveals that if they did not share goods and services with one another, they would often perish</u>. Hunters in the Ache tribe, living in the Paraguayan jungle, for example, have a lot of ups and downs in their daily success at the hunt. Some days they bring home much more food than they could possibly eat; other days they come home empty-handed. ② <u>If a man caught a wild pig and kept it to himself and his family, much of them would go to waste</u> (there are no deep freeze refrigerators in the Paraguayan jungle). During unlucky periods, individual hunters and their families would starve. ③ <u>Instead of living by a philosophy of “rugged individualism,” however, hunters who have a lucky day share their meat with other families</u>. ④ <u>And they don’t just share a little; they share a lot ― fully 90%</u>. ⑤ <u>By exchanging resources in this way, the group provides a mutual insurance policy against starvation</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ② 가 정답입니다. 선행사 **much**는 단수·불가산에 해당하므로 대명사 **it**이 맞고, "much of them"은 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: Resource depletion can be driven by fear that others will use it first. ① <u>Deborah experienced this kind of hoarding when she served as a camp cook for her husband’s geology field camp one summer</u>. Although she did her best to estimate group uses of various foods, within a week in the wilderness area, she realized they were running low on hot chocolate. She hoped that as scarcity became apparent to other camp members, its value would go up and they would be more careful about using it, rationing it till the end. ② <u>Instead, as soon as scarcity became apparent, the remainder of the hot chocolate disappeared quickly</u>, much faster than it was used before scarcity was obvious. Apparently, camp members wanted to be sure they got their share before it completely ran out. Being somewhat of a hoarder herself, Deborah wanted to put the chocolate away, and use it only on special occasions, ③ <u>so that it would lasts</u> longer. Thus simply perceiving a commodity as scarce will increase the demand for it. Restaurant servers use this principle when they strategically hint to their customers that they should preorder the chocolate truffle cake because there aren’t many left and then ④ <u>sell a record-breaking number of the dessert</u>. ⑤ <u>Customers may order more when they believe supply is limited</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 조동사 **would** 뒤에는 동사원형 **last**가 와야 하며, "would lasts"는 어법상 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: A technological innovation represents an opportunity for reduced uncertainty in another sense that is reduced by the information base of the technology, as well as creating one kind of uncertainty about its expected consequences in the mind of potential adopters. ① <u>The former type of potential uncertainty reduction that are from the information embodied in the technological innovation itself, represents the possible efficacy of the innovation in solving an individual’s perceived problem</u>; this advantage provides the motivation that urges an individual to exert effort in order to learn about the innovation. ② <u>Once such information-seeking activities have reduced the uncertainty about the innovation’s expected consequences to a tolerable level for the individual, a decision concerning adoption or rejection will be made</u>. ③ <u>If a new idea is used by an individual, further evaluative information about its effects is obtained</u>. ④ <u>Thus, the innovation-decision process is essentially an information-seeking and information-processing activity in which the individual is motivated to reduce uncertainty about the advantages and disadvantages of the innovation</u>. ⑤ <u>This advantage provides the motivation that urges an individual to exert effort in order to learn about the innovation</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ① 가 정답입니다. 선행사 **type**는 단수이므로 관계절의 동사는 **is**가 맞습니다. "that are from"은 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: ① <u>A technological innovation represents an opportunity for reduced uncertainty in another sense that is reduced by the information base of the technology, as well as creating one kind of uncertainty about its expected consequences in the mind of potential adopters</u>. The former type of potential uncertainty reduction that is from the information embodied in the technological innovation itself, represents the possible efficacy of the innovation in solving an individual’s perceived problem; this advantage provides the motivation that urges an individual to exert effort in order to learn about the innovation. ② <u>Once such information-seeking activities has reduced the uncertainty about the innovation’s expected consequences to a tolerable level for the individual, a decision concerning adoption or rejection will be made</u>. ③ <u>If a new idea is used by an individual, further evaluative information about its effects is obtained</u>. ④ <u>Thus, the innovation-decision process is essentially an information-seeking and information-processing activity in which the individual is motivated to reduce uncertainty about the advantages and disadvantages of the innovation</u>. ⑤ <u>The former type of potential uncertainty reduction that is from the information embodied in the technological innovation itself, represents the possible efficacy of the innovation in solving an individual’s perceived problem; this advantage provides the motivation that urges an individual to exert effort in order to learn about the innovation</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ② 가 정답입니다. 주어 "activities"는 복수이므로 **have reduced**가 맞고, "has reduced"는 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: ① <u>A technological innovation represents an opportunity for reduced uncertainty in another sense that is reduced by the information base of the technology, as well as creating one kind of uncertainty about its expected consequences in the mind of potential adopters</u>. ② <u>The former type of potential uncertainty reduction that is from the information embodied in the technological innovation itself, represents the possible efficacy of the innovation in solving an individual’s perceived problem; this advantage provides the motivation that urges an individual to exert effort in order to learn about the innovation</u>. ③ <u>Once such information-seeking activities have reduced the uncertainty about the innovation’s expected consequences to a tolerable level for the individual, a decision concerning adoption or rejection will be made</u>. ④ <u>If a new idea are used by an individual, further evaluative information about its effects is obtained</u>. ⑤ <u>Thus, the innovation-decision process is essentially an information-seeking and information-processing activity in which the individual is motivated to reduce uncertainty about the advantages and disadvantages of the innovation</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ④ 가 정답입니다. 주어 **a new idea**는 단수이므로 **is used**가 맞고, "are used"는 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: As baseball player Pete Rose once noted, “Baseball is a team game, but nine men who meet their individual goals make a nice team.” ① <u>In baseball, as in cricket and other bat-and-ball games, a team are a loosely integrated confederacy</u>. Individual efforts are mostly independent, seldom involving more than two or three players at a time. ② <u>Particularly on defense, players are separated from one another by significant distance</u>. Because of the differentiated, loosely linked nature of a baseball team, very little coordination is required among the various positions. The pitcher and catcher must each know what the other is going to do, and, at times, in fielders must anticipate how a teammate will act, particularly in a double play or squeeze situation. ③ <u>But a hitter is alone in the batter’s box, and a center fielder is alone in a large expanse</u>. ④ <u>Most managerial decisions are mostly tactical, normally involving individual substitutions or actions</u>. ⑤ <u>Managers stand in the dugout, wearing the team uniform, apparently thinking strategically</u>.
question_data.Explanation: ① 가 정답입니다. 주어 **a team**은 단수이므로 **is**가 맞고, "a team are"는 수 일치가 틀립니다.

question_data.Question: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
question_data.Paragraph: As baseball player Pete Rose once noted, “Baseball is a team game, but nine men who meet their individual goals make a nice team.” In baseball, as in cricket and other bat-and-ball games, a team is a loosely integrated confederacy. ① <u>Individual efforts are mostly independent, seldom involving more than two or three players at a time</u>. Particularly on defense, players are separated from one another by significant distance. Because of the differentiated, loosely linked nature of a baseball team, very little coordination is required among the various positions. ② <u>The pitcher and catcher must each know what the other is going to do</u>, and, at times, in fielders must anticipate how a teammate will act, particularly in a double play or squeeze situation. But a hitter is alone in the batter’s box, and a center fielder is alone in a large expanse. Most managerial decisions are mostly tactical, normally involving individual substitutions or actions. ③ <u>Managers stands in the dugout, wearing the team uniform, apparently thinking strategically</u>. They can come and go without seriously disrupting a team’s play. Players can transfer from one team to another with relative ease, because ④ <u>a newcomer can do the job without major disruption to a team’s style of play</u>. ⑤ <u>John Updike summed it up well: “Of all the team sports, baseball, with its graceful intermittence of action, its immense and tranquil field sparsely salted with poised men in white, and its dispassionate mathematics, seemed to be best suited to accommodate, and be ornamented by, a loner.”</u>
question_data.Explanation: ③ 가 정답입니다. 주어 **Managers**는 복수이므로 동사 **stand**가 맞고, "Managers stands"는 수 일치가 틀립니다.
